content.full_text:
State of California
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. BOX 807 · SACRAMENTO, 95804 · · · 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814

Technical Assistance · · Administration · · Executive/Legal · · Enforcement · · Statements of Economic Interest
(916) 322-5662          322-5660              322-5501              322-6461              322-6444

September 9, 1981

Joseph E. Irvine
Executive Director
Community Airport Council
18103 Sky Park South, Ste. 201
Irvine, CA 92741

A-51-09-051

Dear Mr. Irvine:

Tom Houston, Chairman of the Fair Political Practices
Commission, has asked me to reply to your letter dated August 20,
1981, regarding the campaign disclosure provisions of the
Political Reform Act as they may apply to the Community Airport
Council. Addressing your questions in order:

(1) There are no prohibitions in the Political Reform
Act regarding the amount of contributions you may make to state
or local candidates. However, some cities and counties have
campaign ordinances which limit the amount of contributions which
may be made to local candidates and ballot measures. So I suggest
you contact the local filing officials regarding any restrictions
which may be imposed by the city or county in which you anticipate
making contributions.

(2) It is legal under the Political Reform Act for the
Council to sponsor a fundraising event for a candidate for the
Board of Supervisors.

(3) If the Community Airport Council qualifies as a
"committee" pursuant to Gov. Code Section 82013 (as further explained
in Part I of the Campaign Information Manual), it will have campaign
reporting obligations. Enclosed please find a copy of the Political
Reform Act of 1974 as amended to January 1, 1981, and the Commission's
Campaign Information Manual.

With regard to the fundraising breakfast the Community
Airport Council will sponsor for a Board of Supervisors candidate,
any costs the Council incurs in connection with the breakfast will
be an "in-kind" contribution to the candidate which must be reported
by the candidate. The Council must provide the candidate with all
pertinent information concerning the expenditures it makes. In
addition, the Council will be acting as an intermediary for the

Joseph E. Irvine
September 9, 1981
Page Two


contributions received, and must provide the candidate with the
required information for the reporting of contributions, i.e.,
if a cumulative amount of $100 or more is received from a single
source, the candidate must report the contributor's name, address,
and occupation or if self-employed, name of business. For the
purpose of reporting contributions received, we recommend that
the above information be maintained for all contributions of $25
or more.

I hope this has adequately answered your questions. If
you have any further questions, please contact me at the above
telephone number for the Technical Assistance Division.

Sincerely,

[illegible signature]

Jeanne Pritchard
Political Reform Consultant
